intro.primary: I mostly do front-end development, with a genuine interest in interfaces that are simple to understand, pleasant to use, and clean to evolve.
intro.secondary: I can also set up the back-end layer when the product needs proper integration, especially with Node.js, APIs, and straightforward business logic.
intro.preferences: I prefer interfaces that build confidence immediately: readable, calm, well-organized, and designed for real usage rather than to impress on first scroll.

profile.sectionTitle: Where I can be useful quickly
profile.description: A quick overview of my way of working, what I enjoy doing, and what I can handle on a product.

expertise.title: What I handle well
expertise.workStyleDesc: I'm more comfortable with interfaces that need to be made understandable, calm, and solid than with screens designed to show off. I like when the front-end really helps the product communicate better.

skills.title: Screens and user flows
skills.description: Build pages and flows that read quickly, navigate easily, and work well on both desktop and mobile.

skills.title: Components and UI base
skills.description: Establish reusable building blocks and simple rules to avoid reinventing the same thing on every screen.

skills.title: Front / business integration
skills.description: Connect the interface to APIs, data, and concrete use cases, with Node.js when a simple and readable back layer is needed.

skills.title: Polish and quality
skills.description: Take care of the details that often make the difference: responsiveness, accessibility, reading comfort, and overall consistency.

cvSummary.title: CV Summary
cvSummary.description: The most useful information if you want to quickly understand my profile before opening the full CV.

items.label: KEY STACK
items.value: React, JavaScript, CSS, Node.js, APIs, .NET, Vercel, Firebase

workProcess.sectionTitle: A simple, structured approach focused on usage
workProcess.intro: I enjoy starting with a somewhat unclear need and transforming it into a clearer, more coherent, and easier to maintain journey.

steps.title: Understand the need
steps.description: I start with what really needs to be solved, for whom, and in what order things should be understood.

steps.title: Make usage obvious
steps.description: I pay attention to reading rhythm, hierarchies, and details that make a page more natural to use.

steps.title: Build a solid foundation
steps.description: I prefer well-structured front-end, useful components, and a Node or API layer clear enough to avoid a patchwork effect between screens and data.

steps.title: Go all the way through
steps.description: I care about the finish: responsiveness, keyboard navigation, performance, visual consistency, and when needed, Node/API integration that holds through to build.

techStack.description: The tools I use most often depending on the product type, interface needs, and what needs to be connected in the back.

categories.name: Method / Delivery
tools: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google and other AI tools

projects.description: Projects with different contexts, but with the same intention: make a product simpler to understand, more pleasant to navigate, and more solid on the front-end.

items.name: Dashboard
items.description: Control dashboard to track activity and support decision-making.
items.objective: The goal was not to display more data, but to help read the right information faster.

items.name: HumanWorkForce
items.description: Product-oriented recruitment platform with modern candidate experience.
items.objective: The balance to find was between brand image, flow readability, and a solid front-end base to continue iterating.

items.name: Skillswap
items.objective: The idea was to verify if matching and skills exchange really worked, with a credible enough product to be tested but simple enough to remain iterable.

items.name: Todo App
items.description: Productivity application with authentication, user accounts, and structured front-end base.
items.objective: A project designed to show I can build a complete utility application without overloading the experience.

items.name: CL
items.description: Premium showcase website to strengthen a local artist's online presence.
items.objective: A showcase site designed to serve a strong visual identity without sacrificing readability or navigation simplicity.

items.name: Shop Template
items.description: Reusable e-commerce base to launch a store with pre-built front-end structure.
items.objective: A template project conceived less as a demo than as a credible starting point for an e-commerce need.

items.name: Artist's Page
items.description: Visual portfolio to showcase works and facilitate contact.
items.objective: An artistic portfolio designed to put the works at the center and create a simple, fluid, and immediately understandable visit.

items.name: Model App
items.description: React + Vite starter designed to quickly establish clear front-end architecture.
items.objective: A front-end base designed to avoid throwaway prototypes and save time on initial architecture decisions.

games.description: A small selection of personal creations around level design, interfaces, and video game experimentation.

footer.aboutAvailability: Available to work on product interfaces, clean front-end bases, and projects that need to be easier to use.
